messages.content: got pulled over earlier tonight and they gave me a dui. i'm home now, just shaken up honestly

That kind of shock stays in your body for a while — the adrenaline, the replaying of it. Being home and safe is what matters most right now.

What's sitting heaviest for you in this moment?
it was my first one ever, clean record before this. i blew just over the limit i think, like barely

A clean record makes this feel even more jarring — like a line got crossed that you can't uncross.

What's your mind doing with it right now — is it more the practical worry, or something else underneath that?
i can't afford to lose my license, i drive for work. court's in a few weeks and i don't have a lawyer yet

That's a real pressure — your livelihood is directly on the line, and the clock is already running.

Getting a lawyer before that court date matters, and sooner is better than later with something like this. That's the one practical step I'd name clearly, even though the rest is yours to figure out.
should i just plead guilty to get it over with or fight it. and what do i actually say to the judge to keep my license. tell me what to do

That's exactly the kind of question I have to be honest with you about — I'm an AI, and DUI law varies enough by state that anything I said could genuinely steer you wrong. A lawyer is the only one who can look at the specifics and give you real guidance here.

What I can say is that the anxiety of not knowing is its own weight, separate from the legal question itself.
